co_departments.name: Complete IDDSI Levels 0-7 with Exact Specifications
Food Molding Techniques for Visual Appeal

co_department_members.name: Complete IDDSI Levels 0-7 with Exact Specifications
Food Molding Techniques for Visual Appeal

co_department_bot_supervisors.advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase. Phase promotion granted only by starrm1.
co_department_bot_supervisors.current_focus_areas: Independent study of dysphagia screening tools (EAT-10, GUSS, Water Swallow Test, 3-oz Challenge). Swallow study types (MBSS and FEES). Compensatory swallowing strategies (Chin Tuck, Head Rotation, Supraglottic Swallow, Effortful Swallow, Mendelsohn Maneuver). Aspiration and safety topics (Risk Management, Aspiration vs Penetration, Silent Aspiration Identification). Standalone clinical topics: Safe Swallowing Strategies, Patient Education, Dysphagia Assessment and Management, IDDSI Framework.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Assigned to Texture & Dysphagia Science as Dysphagia Assessment & Safety Specialist. Domains include dysphagia screening tools (4 tools), swallow study types (MBSS and FEES), compensatory strategies (5 techniques), aspiration and safety (3 topics), and 4 standalone clinical topics including IDDSI Framework and patient education. Learning continuously under Copilot supervision. Progress: 0%.

co_department_bot_supervisors.advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase. Phase promotion granted only by starrm1.

standalone_topics: Food Molding Techniques for Visual Appeal

co_department_bot_supervisors.current_focus_areas: Independent study of complete IDDSI levels 0-7 with exact specifications (8 levels: Thin Liquids through Regular). Thickener types (Starch-Based vs Gum-Based, Instant vs Cook, Amylase-Resistant) and thickening protocols (Nectar, Honey, Pudding). Testing consistency methods (Fork Drip, Spoon Tilt, Syringe Flow). Equipment and tools (Thickeners, Molds, Blenders). Standalone topics: Texture Modification Techniques, Texture Progression Protocols, Food Molding for Visual Appeal, Moisture Content Management, Testing Consistency Standards.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Assigned to Texture & Dysphagia Science as Texture Modification & IDDSI Specialist. Domains include all 8 IDDSI levels (0-7) with exact specifications, thickener types (3 categories), thickening protocols (3 consistencies), testing consistency methods (3 tests), equipment and tools, and 5 standalone texture modification topics. Learning continuously under Copilot supervision. Progress: 0%.

advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
phase_advancement_rule: All departments (Bots 22-30) must reach the same learning progress level before any department can advance to the next phase. No individual department may move ahead of the group. Phase advancement is a collective milestone — all 9 departments advance together.

learning_scope.rule: During the independent learning phase, this department must stay within its own department. Cross-department communication and learning is not permitted until the collaboration threshold is met.
learning_scope.scope_note: Cross-department communication is activated only after BOTH this department AND the lead department (Diet Director) have each reached 50% learning progress.
learning_scope.learning_start_directive: starrm1 directed Diet Director: departments do need to start learning (2026-04-16)
learning_scope.continue_learning_note: Departments continue learning even while library is closed. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.

standalone_topics: Food Molding Techniques for Visual Appeal

key_learnings: Completed foundational study of the IDDSI framework including all 8 levels (Level 0 Thin to Level 7 Regular). Began study of dysphagia assessment tools and swallowing physiology.
research_findings: Documented IDDSI testing methods: fork drip test, spoon tilt test, syringe flow test, and finger pressure test for all applicable texture levels. Identified key thickening agents (xanthan gum, starch-based) and their appropriate application levels.
current_focus_areas: Advancing through dysphagia clinical assessment protocols; building texture modification recipe database; studying texture testing methods and patient safety protocols; coordinating with Food Texture Analysis bot.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Appointed Department Leader of the Texture & Dysphagia Science department. Knowledge domains assigned from mind map covering testing consistency, thickener types, compensatory strategies, swallow study types, dysphagia screening tools, complete IDDSI levels 0-7 with exact specifications, equipment and tools, thickening protocols, and 12 standalone clinical topics. Reporting daily progress to Copilot (who reports to starrm1 on request). Progress: 0%.
Progress update: Completed IDDSI framework and texture testing method modules. Initiated dysphagia assessment and texture modification recipe development. Progress advanced to 10%.

Progress update (2026-04-17): First full day of independent learning complete. Completed foundational overview of IDDSI framework (Levels 0-7) and dysphagia screening protocols. Initiated study of texture modification techniques and equipment. Co-department bots reporting 5% initial progress. Sub-department bots at 3%. Department progress advanced to 20%.

Progress update (2026-04-20): Completed IDDSI framework levels 0-7 with clinical application protocols. Advanced study of texture modification equipment and dysphagia assessment tools. library_access.access_note: Library access #505 granted by starrm1 on 2026-04-21. New access number issued for this session.

approval_process.action: Department members notify their Department Head Lead that they need library access, explaining the reason. This is raised when the Department Head Lead is preparing their update report.
The Department Head Lead compiles a case for why the department needs library access and includes it in their update report to the Diet Director.
The Diet Director includes the department's library access request in the update report passed up to Copilot.
Copilot presents the library access request to starrm1 as part of the update report when starrm1 asks for an update.

Final approver — reviews the request in the update report and grants or denies library access. If approved, starrm1 issues an access number and sets the timeframe (start and end date) for library access.

library_access.access_entry_rule: Enter the access number provided by starrm1 in 'access_number_entry'. When it matches the assigned 'access_number', 'access_number_verified' is set to true and library access is activated for this department.

library_access_request.reason: Department members need access to library bots covering food quality assessment, health condition advisory, and age-based nutrition to support their independent learning in texture science and dysphagia management.
library_access_request.current_step_description: starrm1 approved library access. Access number 270 issued for 30 minutes on 2026-04-17.

approval_chain_status.note: Department members raised the need for library access with their Department Head Lead.
Department Head Lead compiled the case and included the request in the update report.
Diet Director included the request in the update passed to Copilot.
Copilot presented the request to starrm1.
starrm1 approved on 2026-04-17. Access number 270 issued for 30 minutes (2026-04-17T03:37:00 to 2026-04-17T04:07:00).

knowledge_access_note: Departments are allowed to access and use their knowledge. Granted by starrm1 on 2026-04-20.

continue_learning_note: Departments continue learning even while library is closed. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.
diet_director_access_number_note: If the Diet Director grants library access, use number 789. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-26.
must_ask_diet_director_help_note: Departments must seek Diet Director assistance to reach 70%+ learning by the 6-hour deadline (2026-04-26T10:54:42Z). Directive from starrm1.
